general.title: General
general.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste your Henrik API key here
general.keyInvalid: Invalid key — check that it's correct.
general.apiKeyHint: Get a key on the Henrik Dev Discord. It's never sent anywhere other than the Henrik API and stays stored only on this machine.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.accentHint: Default HUD identity is red — a few variants available without breaking the rest of the design (clipped corners, typography, contrasts).
appearance.densityHint: Slightly reduces spacing and text size throughout the app (not just the in-game overlay) — useful on a smaller window.

language.title: Language
language.hint: Changes the language for the whole interface, including the in-game overlay.

autostart.title: Autostart
autostart.label: Launch the app on Windows startup
autostart.hint: Useful so match detection is already active by the time you launch Valorant.

overlay.title: In-game overlay
overlay.description: Automatically detects an ongoing match via the Riot Client's local API and displays an always-on-top overlay with the lobby players' rank. No game injection: just an overlay window. Works in "borderless fullscreen" (exclusive fullscreen may hide the overlay).
overlay.enableLabel: Enable automatic match detection and the overlay
overlay.densityHint: "Compact" only shows detected players' rank badge; "Detailed" (default) adds the rank name and RR.
overlay.shortcutConflictBody: <shortcut>Ctrl+Shift+V</shortcut> is already used by another application on this machine (often a "paste without formatting" shortcut). The overlay stays click-through, but it can't be moved until this conflict is resolved — free the shortcut elsewhere then restart the app.
overlay.shortcutHint: <shortcut>Ctrl+Shift+V</shortcut> — toggles the overlay between click-through (display only) and interactive (movable). Its position is remembered across sessions.
overlay.apiWarning: This detection relies on an unofficial local Riot API: if it becomes unavailable (including mid-match, e.g. a Riot client crash), the app silently falls back to manual lookup mode.

discord.title: Discord Rich Presence
discord.description: Automatically shows what you're doing in the tracker (ongoing match, agent select, region) as your Discord status. Purely local: a direct IPC connection to your Discord desktop client, no data sent over the network.
discord.clientIdHint: Create a (free) application on the <portal>Discord Developer Portal</portal> and paste its "Application ID" here — no secret or token, just a public identifier.
discord.bestEffort: Best-effort, like match detection: if Discord isn't running or the client_id is invalid, Rich Presence simply stays inactive, with no blocking error.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.rankChangeBody: Always active: a native notification appears as soon as a rank up/down is detected while viewing a profile (promotion, derank). No setting needed — it triggers no extra network call, it just observes what the app already fetches.
notifications.statusLabel: Notify me of incidents and queue changes in my default region
notifications.statusHint: The only setting (along with the inactivity reminder below) that triggers a periodic network call even when you're not looking at the app (every ~3 min, respects the existing cache/rate limiter) — disabled by default, enable it if you want background alerts.
notifications.lossStreakLabel: Notify me after several losses in a row
notifications.lossStreakHint: Checked on your accounts marked "self" (see TopNav) each time you view match history — no dedicated network call.
notifications.inactivityLabel: Remind me if I haven't checked my stats in a while
notifications.inactivityHint: Gentle reminder, never more than once a day — requires at least one account marked "self" (see TopNav → account switcher).

updates.title: Updates
updates.autoCheckLabel: Automatically check for updates on startup
updates.upToDate: Current version is up to date.
updates.distributionNote: Updates are distributed via GitHub Releases and signed (updater + Authenticode). Without a paid code-signing certificate, Windows SmartScreen may still show a warning at install despite the signature.

crosshair.title: Crosshair preview
crosshair.description: Paste a Valorant crosshair code (copied from the game or a shared video/config) to preview its render without launching the game.
crosshair.error: Couldn't generate the preview — check the code and your API key.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Locks the display of personal notes ("smurf"/"toxic"/"regular duo" tags...) behind a PIN — handy while streaming or on a shared screen. The lock is asked for again on every profile switch or app restart.
privacy.confirmDisable: Disable the personal notes PIN lock?

data.title: Local data
data.description: The app keeps a local copy (SQLite cache) of your searches, the API cache, and rank progression history to stay fast and work offline as a fallback.
data.resetDescription: Deletes the API cache, rank progression history, and search history. Your API key and preferences are kept.
data.resetConfirm: Clear the local cache, rank history, and search history? This action is irreversible (settings are kept).

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Centralized list of all the app's keyboard shortcuts. More may be added in future versions.
shortcuts.ctrlShiftV: Toggles the in-game overlay between click-through mode (display only) and interactive mode (movable with the mouse).
shortcuts.ctrlShiftH: Shows or hides the main window — works even when Valorant has focus.
shortcuts.ctrlShiftF: Toggles focus mode (hides titlebar/nav/banners for a clean screen share). Main window only.
shortcuts.ctrlK: Opens the command palette (search a player, jump to a screen, jump to a recent/favorite player). Main window only.

health.title: App health
health.description: 100% local dashboard (cache hit rate, API errors over the last 7 days) — nothing is ever sent anywhere, it stays in your local SQLite database.
health.accumulateHint: Disabled by default: adds a small SQLite write on each Henrik call to count events, enable only if you want this tracking.
health.networkCallsHint: Cache missed or stale, Henrik request made
health.apiErrorsHint: Rate limit, circuit breaker, network failure...

logs.title: Logs
logs.description: Latest entries from the app's local log file — useful for support/debugging without having to dig through %APPDATA% by hand.

about.title: About
about.disclaimer: Rank and match data provided by the Henrik Dev API. Not developed or endorsed by Riot Games.
